---
title: "Finance Data & AI Portfolio"
subtitle: "Tested models · orchestrated runs · AI for judgment — never the math"
author: "Theerayut (TJ) Jaiyen"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme.scss]
    embed-resources: true     # single self-contained .html — easy to share/present offline
    slide-number: true
    footer: "TJ Jaiyen · linkedin.com/in/jaiyentheerayut"
    transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 720
jupyter: python3
---

## The question every close asks {.center}

> *Which accounts moved, are the moves material, and why?*

A cost accountant who builds the **data, orchestration, and AI layers** behind finance
reporting — with correctness and governance **built in, not bolted on**.

::: notes
Anchor on the business problem first, not the tech. Every month-end close is this one question.
:::

## The approach

- **Tested dbt models** for the numbers — never a spreadsheet
- **Airflow + Cosmos** for the schedule, retries, and recovery
- **An LLM only for the judgment layer** — narrative and exception flags, guardrailed

. . .

The AI touches **judgment and language**. The math is deterministic and tested.

## How it fits together

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  G[raw GL seed] --> V[dbt_finance_variance]
  V --> M[fct_account_variance · tested mart]
  A[Airflow + Cosmos] -.per-model tasks & retries.-> V
  M --> C[Claude variance agent · guardrailed]
  P[GPU events seed] --> X[dbt_gpu_cost_attribution] --> T[gpu_cost_by_tenant]
  M --> S[site/ · client-side sandbox]
```

## dbt_finance_variance — the modeled numbers

Raw GL → typed **staging** → a tested **`fct_account_variance`** mart
(prior-vs-current variance, % change, materiality flag).

- A **relationship test** means the mart can never reference an account absent from the source —
  **data governance enforced in CI**
- Runs locally on **DuckDB**, no warehouse: `dbt build` → **14 tests pass**

## What the mart surfaces {.smaller}

```{python}
#| label: variance-chart
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: center
import pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.read_csv("../dbt_finance_variance/seeds/raw_financials.csv")
piv = df.pivot_table(index="account", columns="period", values="amount")
piv["pct"] = (piv["2026Q2"] - piv["2026Q1"]) / piv["2026Q1"] * 100
top = piv.reindex(piv["pct"].abs().sort_values(ascending=False).index).head(8)[::-1]
colors = ["#c0392b" if v > 0 else "#1e8449" for v in top["pct"]]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.6))
ax.barh(top.index, top["pct"], color=colors)
ax.axvline(0, color="#888", lw=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel("Q1 → Q2 variance (%)"); ax.set_title("Top account movers — prior vs current period")
for s in ["top", "right"]: ax.spines[s].set_visible(False)
for y, v in enumerate(top["pct"]):
    ax.text(v + (3 if v >= 0 else -3), y, f"{v:+.0f}%", va="center",
            ha="left" if v >= 0 else "right", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

Purchase Price Variance and Labor Efficiency Variance are the swings a controller flags first.

## dbt_gpu_cost_attribution — same discipline, AI cloud cost

The cost-accounting method pointed at **AI unit economics**.

- Idle GPU capacity absorbed across tenants **by token share** — the same **overhead-absorption**
  logic that spreads shared factory cost in job-order costing
- Per-tenant **gross margin** + a **margin zone** fall out
- Three test tiers (data, a **unit test** on the allocation math, **singular tests**: ratios sum to 1,
  no negative cost) → **33 tests pass**. It surfaces a **tenant running negative margin**.

## airflow_orchestration — reliable runs

The dbt project rendered into **native Airflow tasks via Astronomer Cosmos**.

- Every seed, model, and test is **its own task** — isolated retries, model-level observability,
  not one opaque `dbt build`
- A downstream task hands the fresh mart to the Claude agent, and **degrades gracefully** with no API key
- CI runs a **DagBag import test** on every push — a broken DAG can't land on `main`

## claude_finance_agent — AI with guardrails

Two periods of financials → a **CFO-ready narrative** + structured exception flags (Anthropic SDK).

- Variances computed **deterministically in code**; the model is used **only for judgment and language**
- A **hallucination guardrail** rejects any output referencing an account not in the source

. . .

> The point isn't "AI writes text" — it's knowing **where AI earns trust** in a close, and where a human must verify.

## site/ — interactive case study {.smaller}

A static **Vite + Tailwind** page tying the projects together for a hiring reviewer, with a
**variance / margin sandbox** running the same math the dbt mart computes — client-side, no backend.
Auto-deploys to **GitHub Pages**.

🔗 **https://tjaiyen.github.io/tj-finance-portfolio/**

## Why these five together {.center}

Two dbt projects for the modeled numbers · Airflow/Cosmos for reliable runs · a guardrailed Claude
agent for the narrative · a site to tie it together.

**Same discipline throughout: tested models, deterministic math, AI only where it earns trust.**

— Theerayut (TJ) Jaiyen · [linkedin.com/in/jaiyentheerayut](https://linkedin.com/in/jaiyentheerayut)

::: notes
Data in all projects is synthetic — no employer or confidential information.
:::
